title: Karpatiosorbus gerecseensis
intro: Karpatiosorbus gerecseensis, also known as Hungarian rowan, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species native to the Carpathian region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Karpatiosorbus gerecseensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory infections, fever, and gastrointestinal issues.
